name: mFLUSIVA / mRNA-1010
target_population: Adults ≥50 years; phase 3 studies include adults ≥18 and ≥65 depending study
availability_signal: Global regulatory submissions reported for US, EU, Canada, and Australia; FDA VRBPAC discussion scheduled June 18, 2026.
notes: Encodes full-length HA antigens for recommended seasonal strains. Lead near-term in-development candidate for Canada/US/EU evidence tracking.

name: mCOMBRIAX / mRNA-1083
availability_signal: EU marketing authorization issued April 20, 2026; launch timing depends on national recommendations and supply decisions.
notes: Combination product contains seasonal influenza HA mRNA components and SARS-CoV-2 spike mRNA components.

name: Pfizer/BioNTech modified mRNA seasonal influenza vaccine
current_stage: Phase 3 completed; active follow-on phase 2 formulation work
target_population: Adults and older adults; adult challenge/formulation studies ongoing or recently completed
availability_signal: Candidate remains active in the pipeline landscape; mixed immune-response findings for B-lineage formulations are a key watch item.
notes: Prior phase 3 evidence showed superior efficacy overall but weaker B-lineage immunogenicity than comparator in published/announced studies.

name: Pfizer/BioNTech mRNA COVID-19 + influenza combination vaccine
target_population: Adults 18–64 and older adults depending study
availability_signal: Phase 3 study met one primary immunogenicity objective but did not meet influenza B non-inferiority objective; sponsors reported formulation evaluation.
notes: Important comparator for combined respiratory vaccination pathways, but not yet a straightforward near-term flu-only availability signal.

name: GSK seasonal influenza mRNA vaccine programme / GSK4382276
target_population: Younger and older adults; additional paediatric and combination studies listed in the pipeline
availability_signal: Positive phase 2 headline data reported; product not yet authorized.
notes: Key evidence needs: phase 3 efficacy, strain-by-strain immune response, safety/reactogenicity, and manufacturing comparability.

name: Sanofi + Novavax non-mRNA COVID-19 + influenza combination candidates
current_stage: Clinical development; FDA Fast Track designation reported
target_population: Adults; specific studies include older adults depending protocol
availability_signal: Two candidates combining Sanofi flu products with Novavax COVID antigen/adjuvant received FDA Fast Track designation in 2024.
notes: Primarily relevant to combined seasonal respiratory vaccination access, not a new standalone influenza antigen platform.

name: Novavax stand-alone influenza vaccine / COVID-19 influenza combination programme
current_stage: Phase 2/3 evidence; available for partnership/no current investment signal
target_population: Adults ≥65 years in initial phase 3 cohort
availability_signal: Robust immune responses reported in initial cohort, but company programme page lists no current investment for flu and combination programmes.
notes: Keep in evidence map because prior NanoFlu evidence and partnership status may affect future availability.

name: OVX836
target_population: Adults and older adults depending phase 2 study
availability_signal: Active phase 2 broad-protection programme; not a seasonal strain-matched vaccine near authorization.
notes: Useful for correlates/mechanism and breadth evidence rather than immediate seasonal product availability.

name: BPL-1357
platform: inactivated whole-virus mixture; intranasal or intramuscular
availability_signal: Recruiting phase 2 challenge study in the United States; not near routine seasonal vaccine availability.
notes: Evidence map should capture mucosal immunity, challenge protection, and breadth as future-season correlates evidence.
